pages.name: Add One Point To A Characteristic

text.content:
Add One Point To A Characteristic

Type: Common

Effects: Add a point to the value of a single Characteristic.

Restriction: STR/DEX/AGI/CON max 11; INT/POW/WP/PER max 13.

Special: May be taken multiple times.

Cost: 1

Acute Senses

Type: Common

Effects: +1 Perception for Characteristic Checks; +30 Notice and Search.

Cost: 1

Artifact

Type: Common

Effects: Possesses a mystical device of significant power.

Special: Additional points increase capabilities.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Ambidextrous

Type: Common

Effects: Uses both hands equally well; only –10 penalty with an additional weapon.

Cost: 1

Increase One Characteristic to Nine

Type: Common

Effects: Sets one Primary Characteristic to 9.

Special: May be taken multiple times.

Cost: 2

Access to One Psychic Discipline

Type: Common

Effects: Grants affinity to one psychic discipline and matrix powers.

Cost: 1

Charm

Type: Common

Effects: Receives positive reactions from strangers.

Special: Limits set by GM.

Cost: 1

Disquieting

Type: Common

Effects: Can make others uneasy at will.

Special: Limits set by GM.

Cost: 1

Animal Affinity

Type: Common

Effects: Gains positive reactions from animals; can communicate basic intentions.

Cost: 1

Danger Sense

Type: Common

Effects: Cannot be surprised unless attacker’s Initiative exceeds his by 150.

Cost: 2

Been Around

Type: Common

Effects: Begins with 50 / 100 / 150 extra XP.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Aptitude in a Subject

Type: Common

Effects: Reduces DP cost of one Secondary Ability by 1.

Restriction: Cannot reduce below 1.

Cost: 1, 2

Access to Natural Psychic Powers

Type: Common

Effects: Use one Psychic Ability instinctively once per minute.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Aptitude in a Field

Type: Common

Effects: Reduces DP cost of an entire field by 1.

Restriction: Cannot reduce below 1.

Cost: 2

Repeat a Characteristics Roll

Type: Common

Effects: Roll one extra die during Characteristic generation and replace one result.

Restriction: Not compatible with method 4.

Special: May be taken multiple times.

Cost: 1

Martial Mastery

Type: Common

Effects: +40 / +80 / +120 Martial Knowledge.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Good Luck

Type: Common

Effects: Reduces fumble range by 1.

Cost: 1

Ki Recovery

Type: Common

Effects: Recovers 1 Ki every 10 minutes; faster with more points.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Jack of All Trades

Type: Common

Effects: No –30 penalty for untrained abilities; +10 to all Secondary Abilities.

Cost: 2

Natural Armor

Type: Common

Effects: Natural Armor 2 vs non‑energy attacks.

Cost: 1

Mystical Armor

Type: Common

Effects: Natural Armor 4 vs energy attacks.

Cost: 1

Untiring

Type: Common

Effects: +3 Fatigue; +6 or +9 with more points.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Uncommon Size

Type: Common

Effects: Increase or decrease Size by up to 5.

Cost: 1

Starting Wealth

Type: Common

Effects: Gain 2,000 / 5,000 / 10,000 GC in starting wealth.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Regeneration

Type: Common

Effects: Increase Regeneration by 2 / 4 / 6 levels.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Elan

Type: Common

Effects: Gain Elan 25 / 45 / 60 with a chosen entity.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Immunity to Pain and Fatigue

Type: Common

Effects: Penalties from pain and Fatigue are halved.

Cost: 1

The Gift

Type: Common

Effects: Can see and use magic; +10 MR.

Cost: 2

See Supernatural

Type: Common

Effects: Can see supernatural things; ignores blinded penalty in such cases.

Cost: 1

Night Vision

Type: Common

Effects: Ignores penalties from darkness (except magical/absolute).

Cost: 1

Fortunate

Type: Common

Effects: Never suffers negative effects determined solely by chance.

Cost: 1

pages.name: Free Access to Any Psychic Discipline

text.content:
Free Access to Any Psychic Discipline

Type: Common

Effects: May use any psychic discipline with PP.

Cost: 2

Quick Reflexes

Type: Common

Effects: +25 / +45 / +60 Initiative.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Learning

Type: Common

Effects: Gain +3 / +6 / +9 XP per session.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Natural Learner

Type: Common

Effects: +10 / +20 / +30 per level in one Secondary Ability.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Natural Learner (Field)

Type: Common

Effects: +5 / +10 per level to all abilities in a field.

Cost: 2, 3

Exceptional Magic Resistance

Type: Common

Effects: +25 / +50 MR.

Cost: 1, 2

Exceptional Physical Resistance

Type: Common

Effects: +25 / +50 PhR, VR, DR.

Cost: 1, 2

Exceptional Psychic Resistance

Type: Common

Effects: +25 / +50 PsR.

Cost: 1, 2

Light Sleeper

Type: Common

Effects: Only –20 Notice penalty while sleeping.

Cost: 1

Elemental Compatibility

Type: Magic

Effects: +20 MA and MR in one magical path; –20 in the opposed path.

Cost: 1

Natural Knowledge of a Path

Type: Magic

Effects: Grants innate knowledge of a Path at level 40 without spending Magic Levels.

Special: May be taken multiple times.

Cost: 1

Contested Spell Mastery

Type: Magic

Effects: +50 to Collision rolls against another beam.

Cost: 1

Aptitude for Magic Development

Type: Magic

Effects: +3 INT for determining spell potential only.

Cost: 1

Half-Attuned to the Tree

Type: Magic

Effects: +20 MA/MR in five Paths of a Tree segment; –20 in the rest.

Restriction: Necromancy excluded.

Cost: 2

Improved Innate Magic

Type: Magic

Effects: Innate spells gain +10 / +20 / +30 potential.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Unspoken Casting

Type: Magic

Effects: Cast spells silently without MA penalty.

Cost: 1

Gestureless Casting

Type: Magic

Effects: No MA penalty when unable to gesture.

Cost: 1

Superior Magic Recovery

Type: Magic

Effects: Regenerates Zeon at 2× / 3× / 4× normal rate.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Amplify Sustained Power

Type: Psychic

Effects: Maintained powers count as one difficulty level higher.

Cost: 2

Psychic Point Recovery

Type: Psychic

Effects: Recover 1 PP every 10 / 5 / 1 minute.

Cost: 1, 2, 3

Psychic Fatigue Resistance

Type: Psychic

Effects: Does not lose Fatigue when failing powers after PP is depleted (except 3rd level).

Cost: 2

Passive Concentration

Type: Psychic

Effects: Can concentrate while performing active actions.

Cost: 2

Psychic Inclination

Type: Psychic

Effects: Gains +1 difficulty level when using powers of one discipline.

Cost: 2

Focus

Type: Psychic

Effects: PP spent to improve Psychic Projection give +20 instead of +10.

Cost: 1

Extreme Concentration

Type: Psychic

Effects: Doubles concentration bonuses (e.g., +20 instead of +10).

Cost: 2

The final step before a player begins to spend DP and complete a character is to allocate Creation Points on Advantages and Disadvantages. As with each creature that populates the world, Anima characters have several idiosyncrasies not solely reflected in their Characteristics and Abilities. These idiosyncrasies are inherently tied to an individual’s essence or nature. They are not learned and do not improve; characters are either born with them, or they never have them.

Is my character ambidextrous, or does he have magical abilities? Was she born with psychic gifts, or does she heal slowly? The answers to these questions cannot rest solely on chance, nor on the whim of each player’s desires.

Each character can acquire Advantages and Disadvantages from the following lists. Each Advantage or Disadvantage grants benefits or inconveniences as described below. Players can spend up to 3 Creation Points (CP) on the Advantages that he chooses and believes most appropriate. The cost of the Advantage indicates how many CP a player must spend to gain it. Some Advantages have variable costs, which mean that they grant greater benefits according to the number of CP spent on them.

Disadvantages function in a similar manner, but instead of costing a player CP, each Disadvantage gives a player Creation Points he can spend on other Advantages. In this way the benefits and inconveniences balance out: The more advantages you have, the greater your problems will be. Players can choose up to three Disadvantages for a character – although they don’t have to choose any.

Unless specifically stated otherwise, players may choose each Advantage or Disadvantage only once.

Common Advantages

Players may choose the following Advantages regardless of a character’s class or race. Cost indicates the number of Creation Points needed to purchase each Advantage.

Magic Advantages

The following Advantages can be acquired only if the character has the Gift of magic. It is not necessary that the character be a member of a class within the Mystic Archetype; anyone with the capacity to weave spells can acquire these benefits.

@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.eVz2stUcanjg1MuA]{Elemental Compatibility}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.7IYLuwlpvaHC0mt1]{Natural Knowledge of a Path}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.ooEnIx88tcnsHUqN]{Contested Spell Mastery}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.k0AWfa4pWYFNld2N]{Aptitude for Magic Development}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.xuNMraWMzRxuiOZK]{Half-Attuned to the Tree}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.yWsswGSqFIk9KYu5]{Improved Innate Magic}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.JiPhv9aS6Cjdepr3]{Unspoken Casting}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.tOGOpPcxaanUhMXI]{Gestureless Casting}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.KPlqr6I3heGoIPk0]{Superior Magic Recovery}

Psychic Adavantages

A character who has previously acquired an Advantage that gives him access to psychic disciplines may choose from the following list:

@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.RgSRV1Jj6WkVDKjw]{Amplify Sustained Power}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.ObMrhkKhv5uw3hCO]{Psychic Point Recovery}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.9UMleJ0jEh44wqcR]{Psychic Fatigue Resistance}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.9l3kK9vwadMEdXfX]{Passive Concentration}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.zqDfYYsplL65Grob]{Psychic Inclination}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.prb13Vop1aF7cBOj]{Focus}@UUID[Compendium.abf-system.abf-journals.JournalEntry.hw9DMaWeFLfiifJ3.JournalEntryPage.WrEyuN14clI61fRs]{Extreme Concentration}
